process_name: Tau to three muons charged-lepton-flavor-violating four-lepton decay
implementation_difficulty_reason: A new four-lepton/contact LFV observable and lepton-sector Wilson-coefficient convention are needed. A first bound does not require lattice inputs or hadronic long-distance calculations, but correlations with dipoles and other LFV modes must be specified.

required_grep_commands: rg -n "tau.?->|tau.?to|taumu|tau.*3.?mu|3.?mu|mu.?mu.?mu|LFV|lepton.?flavo.?r|muToEGamma|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
negative_result: No tau->3mu, tau four-lepton, or charged-lepton contact LFV implementation found in required code paths.
The only LFV implementation surfaced by grep is the adjacent mu->e gamma dipole checker.
adjacent_noncoverage_evidence: scanParams/scan.py:524 calls check_mu_to_e_gamma in the scan.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 lists only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 systems.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:}
\(\mathcal{B}(\tau^- \to \mu^-\mu^+\mu^-)\).
This entry covers the charged-lepton-flavor-violating four-lepton decay
\(\tau^- \to \mu^-\mu^+\mu^-\), including charge-conjugate modes when
experiments quote combined limits.  It is the contact-operator counterpart to
the tau dipole searches in the \texttt{charged\_lepton} family.
sections.pdg_prose:
The current PDG tau listing gives
\[
  \mathcal{B}(\tau^- \to \mu^-\mu^+\mu^-) < 1.9\times 10^{-8}
  \quad (90\%~{\rm CL}),
\]
with the PDG-selected entry attributed to the Belle II ADACHI 24R result
using \(424~{\rm fb}^{-1}\) of \(e^+e^-\) data
(\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.primary\_current\_limit}).  The local
PDG extract is
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/L009/pdg\_2025\_tau\_listing.txt}.
The Belle II primary paper reports the same \(1.9\times 10^{-8}\) 90\% CL
limit (\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.primary\_experiment}).  A newer
LHCb Run 2 preprint, not yet part of that PDG listing, also sets
\(1.9\,(2.3)\times 10^{-8}\) at 90\% (95\%) CL with \(5.4~{\rm fb}^{-1}\) of
\(13~{\rm TeV}\) \(pp\) data
(\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.post\_pdg\_update}).  Historical
benchmarks are Belle 2010 \(<2.1\times 10^{-8}\), BaBar 2010
\(<3.3\times 10^{-8}\), and LHCb 2015 \(<4.6\times 10^{-8}\), all at 90\% CL
(\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.historical\_experimental\_limits[0]},
\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.historical\_experimental\_limits[1]},
and \texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.historical\_experimental\_limits[2]}).
sections.relevance:
This mode tests lepton-sector flavor violation that need not be dipole
dominated.  In warped or composite-flavor extensions, flavor-nonuniversal
lepton profiles, KK gauge exchange, flavor-changing \(Z\) couplings, or
scalar exchange can generate effective \(\tau\mu\mu\mu\) operators.  The
observable is therefore complementary to \(\tau\to\mu\gamma\) and
\(\mu\to e\gamma\): dipoles can feed the three-muon final state through an
off-shell photon, but the four-lepton final state is also directly sensitive
to contact LFV.
sections.post_2008:
Relative to the arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline, the experimental
frontier moved to \(10^{-8}\)-level limits
(\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.rs\_context};
\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.historical\_experimental\_limits}).  Belle
and BaBar published full three-lepton tau searches in 2010, reaching
\(2.1\times 10^{-8}\) and \(3.3\times 10^{-8}\), respectively
(\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.historical\_experimental\_limits[0]} and
\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.historical\_experimental\_limits[1]}).
LHCb demonstrated a hadron-collider tau-to-three-muons program, setting
\(4.6\times 10^{-8}\) with Run 1 data and \(1.9\times 10^{-8}\) with Run 2
(\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.historical\_experimental\_limits[2]};
\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.post\_pdg\_update}).  Belle II now
supplies the PDG benchmark, \(1.9\times 10^{-8}\)
(\texttt{L009.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent.primary\_current\_limit}).
sections.validity:
Experimentally this is a clean upper limit on a forbidden charged-LFV decay.
As a constraint on the current quark-scan code it is lepton-extension-only:
the present quark Delta F=2 pipeline has no direct tau-lepton matching.  As a
future RS constraint, the translation is model dependent because a dipole-only
assumption, a contact-operator assumption, and a full lepton-sector EFT can
give different correlations with \(\tau\to\mu\gamma\), \(\mu\to e\gamma\), and
\(\mu\to 3e\).
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Targeted greps over \texttt{quarkConstraints/},
\texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/},
\texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} found no implementation of
\(\tau\to3\mu\), tau four-lepton LFV, or contact LFV.  The adjacent LFV code is
only \(\mu\to e\gamma\): \texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:1},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}, and \texttt{scanParams/scan.py:524}.
The modern phenomenology surface is neutral-meson mixing only, see
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{MEDIUM.}  A first implementation needs a new four-lepton LFV observable
and a convention for lepton-flavor Wilson coefficients or RS matching.  It
does not require lattice inputs or hadronic long-distance treatment, but a
production-grade implementation should decide whether to include dipole/contact
interference and correlations with the existing \(\mu\to e\gamma\) lane.

sources.block_key: Branching fraction upper limit for tau^- -> mu^- mu+ mu^-

priority_rationale_short: Tau->3mu probes contact LFV, sub-leading but complementary.
priority_rationale_full:
tau->3mu is a clean charged-LFV limit and is sensitive to four-lepton/contact
operators, not only dipoles. Its current 1.9e-8 reach is far behind rare-muon
LFV for generic anarchic lepton flavor. It is still MEDIUM because tau-sector
misalignment can be less tied to mu->e gamma in extended models.
